rows.reason: The rationale explicitly discounts the serious fraction as non-diagnostic and correctly bases the signal on the pharmacologically plausible, well-established statin risks of myalgia and dysglycemia.

The rationale explicitly sets aside the high serious_fraction and instead grounds its signal on specific warfarin-consistent reactions (raised INR, interactions, falls) while appropriately noting causation limits.
The rationale explicitly disclaims the serious fraction and grounds the signal in specific, clustered, mechanistically plausible reactions matching isotretinoin's known profile.
The rationale correctly ties non-specific reactions to cetirizine's known profile and explicitly discounts the 0.55 serious fraction as a FAERS reporting artifact rather than treating it as a signal.
The rationale accurately reflects the absence of any records and correctly concludes no signal can be assessed without relying on serious_fraction.
